title: Air Fryer Sriracha & Honey Halloumi Chicken
summary: Stuck for meal inspiration? Try this easy, quick chicken dish that will hit the spot every time giving you a Nando’s fakeaway feel.

method.description: Use a sharp knife to cut 3 deep slits into each of the chicken breasts, making sure not to slice all the way through. Rub the Cajun seasoning all over the chicken. Slice the halloumi into 1cm thick slices. Stuff the slits in the chicken breast with halloumi.
Put the sriracha and honey into a bowl and mix well. Brush on top of the chicken breasts.

Cook in the air fryer at 190°C for 13-14 mins or until the chicken is cooked through (you may need an extra few mins if using large chicken breasts). If you don’t want your halloumi too dark or crispy, turn the halloumi halfway through the cooking time and baste.
Meanwhile, put the dipping sauce ingredients into a small bowl. Mix well.
Serve the chicken with the dipping sauce, a side salad and rice for a delicious yet simple dinner.

chefs.name: Nathan Anthony
chefs.summary: Meet Nathan Anthony, the Northern Irish home cook behind Bored of Lunch. During lockdown, he started his food blog to others to cook and eat healthier giving everyone something positive to focus on. Specialising in easy, budget-friendly recipes, Nathan’s platform boasts over 4 million online followers and subscribers. With four bestselling cookbooks already under his belt, Nathan’s journey with Bored of Lunch has been nothing short of remarkable. Join the BOL family and explore his delicious recipes across the website, cookbooks, and social media channels.
